title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
description: A shocking psychological thriller of a woman's act of violence against her husband, and of the therapist obsessed with uncovering her hidden motive.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship for writing robust, maintainable code. It provides practical advice on naming, refactoring, and structuring functions.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: A landmark volume in science writing exploring the depths of the universe. Hawking breaks down complex concepts like black holes, time travel, and the big bang.

title: Where the Crawdads Sing
author: Delia Owens
description: A coming-of-age murder mystery set in the quiet marshes of North Carolina. It beautifully intertwines a tale of isolation with a gripping courtroom drama.

title: JavaScript: The Good Parts
author: Douglas Crockford
description: Unearthing the excellence in JavaScript to create truly effective web applications. Crockford isolates the reliable features to help developers write elegant code.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: An evolutionary perspective on genetics and the biological drive to survive. Dawkins introduces the concept of memes and explains evolution from a gene-centric view.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: A sweeping science fiction epic of politics, religion, and survival on a desert planet. Paul Atreides must navigate deadly factions to control the universe's most valuable resource.

title: You Don't Know JS
author: Kyle Simpson
description: A deep dive into the core mechanisms of the JavaScript language. This foundational guide covers scopes, closures, and the intricacies of the JS engine.

title: Cosmos
author: Carl Sagan
description: A journey through space and time, exploring our place in the universe. Sagan eloquently connects the history of scientific discovery with the vastness of the cosmos.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A philosophical tale about a young shepherd's journey to find his personal legend. It teaches readers about listening to their hearts and recognizing opportunity.

title: Design Patterns
author: Erich Gamma
description: Elements of reusable object-oriented software. The 'Gang of Four' provide solutions to common software design problems using tested patterns.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A brief history of humankind, exploring how biology and history have defined us. Harari covers the cognitive, agricultural, and scientific revolutions that shaped modern humans.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: An easy and proven way to build good habits and break bad ones. Clear offers a practical framework for improving every day through small, incremental changes.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: A groundbreaking tour of the mind, explaining the two systems that drive the way we think: System 1 is fast and emotional; System 2 is slower and logical.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt
description: A classic programming book that focuses on the core process of software development independent of any particular language, emphasizing best practices and career development.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian social science fiction novel and cautionary tale about the dangers of totalitarianism, mass surveillance, and repressive regimentation of persons and behaviours within society.

title: The Lean Startup
author: Eric Ries
description: How today's entrepreneurs use continuous innovation to create radically successful businesses. It provides a scientific approach to creating and managing startups.

title: Astrophysics for People in a Hurry
author: Neil deGrasse Tyson
description: A clear and concise introduction to the universe, covering complex topics like quantum mechanics, black holes, and the search for extraterrestrial life in easily digestible chapters.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A novel about the serious issues of rape and racial inequality told through the eyes of young Scout Finch in the Deep South.

title: Grit
author: Angela Duckworth
description: The power of passion and perseverance. Duckworth shows anyone striving to succeed that the secret to outstanding achievement is not talent, but a special blend of passion and persistence.

title: Cracking the Coding Interview
author: Gayle Laakmann McDowell
description: 189 programming questions and solutions. A comprehensive guide to software engineering interviews, covering data structures, algorithms, and system design.

title: Good to Great
author: Jim Collins
description: Why some companies make the leap... and others don't. Collins details the findings of a five-year study to uncover the key determinants of greatness in business.

title: The Power of Habit
author: Charles Duhigg
description: Why we do what we do in life and business. An exploration of the science behind habit creation and reformation.

title: The Immortal Life of Henrietta Lacks
author: Rebecca Skloot
description: The story of a poor Southern tobacco farmer whose cells—taken without her knowledge in 1951—became one of the most important tools in medicine.

title: Steve Jobs
author: Walter Isaacson
description: The exclusive biography of the driven, creative, and sometimes abrasive co-founder of Apple, based on more than forty interviews with Jobs himself.
